body.title: CLOSED: Bound Cortex._mem_cache to LRU with size cap
body.result: OrderedDict + _MEM_CACHE_MAX=5000 cap; LRU eviction skips genesis; cache_stats(); 5 tests — commit 7428c136
body.description:
Cortex._mem_cache is a plain dict with TTL-only eviction (cortex.py:968). TTL only fires when the same id is fetched again — fetch-once-and-never-revisit entries sit forever. Under a 72h session with heavy reading this is a slow monotonic leak. Fix: swap dict for OrderedDict + _MEM_CACHE_MAX = int(os.getenv('IGOR_MEM_CACHE_MAX', '5000')). In _cache_put: if over cap, pop oldest non-genesis entry first. Add cache_stats() metric. Genesis-immortal behavior unchanged.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (lines 968, _cache_put, _cache_get)
**Design rules:** none-apply (implementation detail)
**Scope boundary:** IN: add size cap + LRU eviction. OUT: do not change genesis-immortal behavior, TTL logic, or any caller.
**Test plan:** add one test: insert 6000 entries with non-genesis type, assert len(_mem_cache) <= 5000; genesis entries survive eviction.
**Pass condition:** After inserting 6000 non-genesis entries, cache size is bounded at 5000; pytest passes.
